arguments.task: Open Help Scout official documentation and official website pages to research API availability, authentication methods, rate limits, pricing or plan requirements, and any developer documentation. Visit first-party pages such as developer docs, API docs, authentication docs, pricing, and support docs. Also check official GitHub or MCP-related official pages if linked from first-party sources. Extract URLs and concise findings only; do not use third-party blogs unless first-party sources are unavailable.

raw_result.description: Customer support platform with inbox, knowledge base, chat, and embedded support APIs.
raw_result.auth_notes: Help Scout documents OAuth 2.0 for its Mailbox API, including Authorization Code for third-party apps and Client Credentials for internal integrations; apps are created in My Apps to obtain client credentials.
raw_result.gating_reason: API access is not available on the Free plan; docs indicate API access on Standard, Plus, and Pro plans, with some endpoints such as Reporting restricted to Plus and Pro.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST APIs include Inbox API 2.0, Docs API, and Webhooks; coverage appears substantial for conversations, customers, mailboxes, knowledge base content, and events, though some endpoint families are plan-restricted.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Help Scout developer and GitHub pages reviewed showed SDKs, app templates, and mobile SDKs, but no verified official MCP server or MCP registry listing was found in the available first-party evidence.

raw_result.blocker: A toolkit can be built from the documented public REST APIs, but production access requires a paid Help Scout plan because the Free plan excludes API access, and some endpoints are restricted by plan.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Rate limits are account-wide and plan-based: Standard 200 requests/minute, Plus 400/minute, Pro 800/minute. Write requests (POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH) count as 2 requests. Docs mention headers including X-RateLimit-Limit-Minute, X-RateLimit-Remaining-Minute, and X-RateLimit-Retry-After.
raw_result.notable_signals: Help Scout maintains a dedicated developer portal, publishes OAuth and rate-limit docs, exposes multiple API families plus webhooks, and has an official GitHub organization with SDKs and app templates.
raw_result.human_review_reason: No official MCP server or registry entry was verified from first-party evidence, so MCP existence is marked false based on available evidence rather than an exhaustive ecosystem search.

result.description: Customer support platform with inbox, knowledge base, chat, and embedded support APIs.
result.auth_notes: Help Scout documents OAuth 2.0 for its Mailbox API, including Authorization Code for third-party apps and Client Credentials for internal integrations; apps are created in My Apps to obtain client credentials.
result.gating_reason: API access is not available on the Free plan; docs indicate API access on Standard, Plus, and Pro plans, with some endpoints such as Reporting restricted to Plus and Pro.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST APIs include Inbox API 2.0, Docs API, and Webhooks; coverage appears substantial for conversations, customers, mailboxes, knowledge base content, and events, though some endpoint families are plan-restricted.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Help Scout developer and GitHub pages reviewed showed SDKs, app templates, and mobile SDKs, but no verified official MCP server or MCP registry listing was found in the available first-party evidence.

result.blocker: A toolkit can be built from the documented public REST APIs, but production access requires a paid Help Scout plan because the Free plan excludes API access, and some endpoints are restricted by plan.
result.rate_limit_notes: Rate limits are account-wide and plan-based: Standard 200 requests/minute, Plus 400/minute, Pro 800/minute. Write requests (POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH) count as 2 requests. Docs mention headers including X-RateLimit-Limit-Minute, X-RateLimit-Remaining-Minute, and X-RateLimit-Retry-After.
result.notable_signals: Help Scout maintains a dedicated developer portal, publishes OAuth and rate-limit docs, exposes multiple API families plus webhooks, and has an official GitHub organization with SDKs and app templates.
result.human_review_reason: No official MCP server or registry entry was verified from first-party evidence, so MCP existence is marked false based on available evidence rather than an exhaustive ecosystem search.
